title: Kangaroo fresh run reaches unstable 200-point policy
summary: A from-scratch Kangaroo outer-loop run using the revised prompt/logger architecture recovered a 200-point policy without resuming from the previous best, but it did not improve beyond the known first-reward plateau. A 32-episode 10000-step follow-up averaged 93.75, showing the policy is not robust enough for promotion.

context.run_type: fresh from scratch; no resume-from-results and no copied previous best

policy_v1.py.diagnosis: First reward suppressed; fire_action_rate=0.0 and first_reward_suppressed_pattern=1.0.

policy_v2.py.diagnosis: Recovered first reward and became the best fresh-run policy, but still only one score event.

policy_v3.py.diagnosis: Showed some search-time promise but did not beat policy_v2 in full evaluation.

policy_v4.py.diagnosis: Used FIRE/punch but did not stabilize the first reward or improve route progress.

policy_v5.py.diagnosis: High FIRE/punch rate but no score events, still shallow upward progress.

interpretation: Starting fresh avoids direct inheritance from the previous best but still converges to the same first-reward ceiling.
The revised architecture can now recover the first RIGHTFIRE reward from scratch, which is useful evidence, but it has not solved Kangaroo's post-200 route.
Kangaroo remains exploratory and should not be added to canonical covered games.
Further reward-only Kangaroo runs are likely to spend budget rediscovering or destabilizing the 200-point behavior unless the objective or scaffold changes.

rq_mapping.RQ1: Adds evidence that the current LeGPS prompt can produce a nonzero Kangaroo policy from scratch, but not a robust or high-performing one.
rq_mapping.RQ2: Shows the current best fresh policy is not punch farming and does not suppress the first reward, but still only achieves a shallow first-event route.
rq_mapping.RQ4: Shows CMA-ES can tune parameters to recover the first reward when the structure exposes it, but does not solve post-200 routing with reward-only search.

recommended_next_steps: Either pause Kangaroo and move to broader game coverage, or introduce an explicitly documented Kangaroo scaffold such as objective shaping or staged subtask evaluation.
If continuing Kangaroo, evaluate whether a shaped objective can optimize upward progress and route behavior without hiding that it is no longer pure reward-only optimization.
